title: [agentmail-fd-pressure] daemon FD exhaustion under reservation traffic — reproducer + plist ulimit bump

did_gates.gate: AG1: build agentmail-fd-pressure-probe.sh with concurrent reservation traffic + lsof FD sampling
AG2: sample idle/single/burst4/burst8/sustained baselines + capture peak/headroom
did_gates.evidence: baselines.json: 5 series all peaking at 14 FDs (0.34% of soft limit); heavy-stress.json: 16-worker × 10s also peaks at 14 FDs

did_gates.gate: AG3: route per decision tree (>70 bump / 40-70 monitor / <40 benign)
did_gates.evidence: Worst-case peak = 0.34%, far below 40% threshold; verdict=benign_no_action; verdict_route=none (NO route to skillos:1, ulimit bump unnecessary)
doctor.json: invariant=agentmail_fd_count_under_pressure, status=pass, threshold_pct=85, fd_pct=0.34; --doctor mode in same probe binary

no_bead_reason: AG3 decision tree returned benign_no_action; no follow-up work warranted; doctor invariant in place to catch future regressions

L61_ecosystem_touch.no_touch_reason: probe + doctor invariant; canonical decision-tree thresholds (70/40/85%) live in --info decision_thresholds, not in canonical doctrine. Doctor invariant promotion to canonical surface is a separate Petal-9 decision.

mission_rationale: Closes the cross-orch storage-FD-blocker question with measurement; replaces speculation with a probe + decision tree + doctor invariant. Removes a false blocker from cross-orch coordination flow and leaves a doctor invariant for future regression detection. Serves continuous-orchestrator-uptime by clearing one active cross-orch blocker and grounding future decisions in data.
